title: Churma Ladoo

ingredients.title: For the Churma Base
items: Whole wheat flour – 2 cups (atta, preferably stone-ground)
Milk – ½ cup (as needed to knead)

instruction.title: Prepare the Dough
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine wheat flour, semolina, and melted ghee. Rub well until mixture feels like breadcrumbs.
Add milk gradually and knead into a stiff dough.
Divide into small, thick oblong rolls (like small cylinders).

instruction.title: Fry the Dough
steps: Heat ghee in a kadhai (medium heat).
Fry the dough rolls on low heat until deep golden brown and crisp.
Drain on paper towels and cool completely.

instruction.title: Grind into Churma
steps: Once cooled, break fried rolls and grind into a coarse powder (not too fine).
Sieve lightly to remove extra fine flour.

instruction.title: Prepare the Jaggery Mixture
steps: In a pan, melt ghee and add jaggery.
Heat gently until jaggery melts (do not boil).
Add cardamom, nutmeg, and roasted nuts.

instruction.title: Mix & Shape Ladoos
steps: Combine churma powder with the jaggery-ghee mixture.
Roll into medium-sized ladoos using greased palms.
Garnish each with a pistachio slice or a sprinkle of roasted poppy seeds.

chefTips: Texture Secret: Add semolina for a subtle crunch that feels luxurious.
Flavor Boost: Roast nuts in ghee, not dry, for deeper aroma.
Premium Touch: Use desi ghee and organic jaggery for the best flavor.
Presentation: Serve in silver foiled paper cups, with a touch of edible silver leaf (varq) for restaurant finesse.
Pairing: Often paired with dal baati in Rajasthan, but in restaurants, serve warm with a drizzle of saffron-infused milk.
